name: Tabs
description: An accessible tab interface that organizes content into selectable panels. Built on Base UI Tabs with StyleX styling.
anatomy:
<Tabs.Root>
  <Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Tab value="a">Tab A</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab value="b">Tab B</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Indicator />
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Panel value="a">Panel A</Tabs.Panel>
  <Tabs.Panel value="b">Panel B</Tabs.Panel>
</Tabs.Root>

Root.description: The Tabs container. Provides state and orientation context.

value.description: The value of the currently active tab (controlled).

onValueChange.description: Callback when the active tab changes.

orientation.description: Layout flow direction. Affects keyboard nav and indicator orientation.

dataAttributes.data-orientation: Reflects the orientation of the tabs.

List.description: Container for the tab buttons. Renders with role="tablist".

activationMode.description: Whether tabs activate as they receive focus (automatic) or only on Enter/Space (manual).

loopFocus.description: Whether arrow-key focus loops at the ends of the list.

Tab.description: An individual tab button. Renders with role="tab" and is wired to its panel via aria-controls/aria-selected.

value.description: The value identifying this tab. Matches Tabs.Panel value.

dataAttributes.data-selected: Present when the tab is active.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the tab is disabled.

Panel.description: The content panel for a tab. Renders with role="tabpanel".

value.description: The value of the tab this panel belongs to.

keepMounted.description: Keep the panel mounted in the DOM when hidden.

dataAttributes.data-hidden: Present when the panel is hidden.

Indicator.description: An animated visual indicator that aligns to the active tab. Reads --active-tab-left/-top/-width/-height CSS vars from Base UI.

renderBeforeHydration.description: Render before React hydrates to minimize SSR flicker.

intents.reasoning: Tabs is the canonical component for switching between mutually exclusive content panels at the same level of hierarchy.
intents.composition:
<Tabs.Root defaultValue="overview">
  <Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Tab value="overview">Overview</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab value="details">Details</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Indicator />
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Panel value="overview">Overview content</Tabs.Panel>
  <Tabs.Panel value="details">Details content</Tabs.Panel>
</Tabs.Root>

intents.reasoning: Vertical Tabs work well for settings UIs where users move between configuration groups.
intents.composition:
<Tabs.Root orientation="vertical" defaultValue="profile">
  <Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Tab value="profile">Profile</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab value="account">Account</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Indicator />
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Panel value="profile">Profile fields</Tabs.Panel>
  <Tabs.Panel value="account">Account fields</Tabs.Panel>
</Tabs.Root>

intents.reasoning: Use manual activation when tab content includes form controls, so screen readers don't trigger panel changes during arrow-key navigation.
intents.composition:
<Tabs.Root defaultValue="step1">
  <Tabs.List activationMode="manual">
    <Tabs.Tab value="step1">Step 1</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab value="step2">Step 2</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Indicator />
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Panel value="step1">{/* form fields */}</Tabs.Panel>
  <Tabs.Panel value="step2">{/* form fields */}</Tabs.Panel>
</Tabs.Root>

avoidWhen.scenario: Sequential steps that must be completed in order
avoidWhen.reasoning: Tabs imply free navigation between siblings. For ordered flows, use a stepper or wizard pattern.
Tabs are for content within a single view. Use NavigationMenu for top-level site nav.

avoidWhen.scenario: Many sections (more than ~7) or long labels that wrap
avoidWhen.reasoning: Tab lists work best with a small number of short labels. Use Accordion or a sidebar nav for larger sets.

examples.name: basic
examples.description: Basic horizontal tabs with an animated indicator.
examples.code:
<Tabs.Root defaultValue="overview">
  <Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Tab value="overview">Overview</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab value="details">Details</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab value="activity">Activity</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Indicator />
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Panel value="overview">Overview content</Tabs.Panel>
  <Tabs.Panel value="details">Details content</Tabs.Panel>
  <Tabs.Panel value="activity">Activity content</Tabs.Panel>
</Tabs.Root>

examples.name: vertical
examples.description: Vertical tabs suitable for settings panels.
examples.code:
<Tabs.Root orientation="vertical" defaultValue="profile">
  <Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Tab value="profile">Profile</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab value="account">Account</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Indicator />
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Panel value="profile">Profile fields</Tabs.Panel>
  <Tabs.Panel value="account">Account fields</Tabs.Panel>
</Tabs.Root>

examples.name: manual-activation
examples.description: Manual activation: focus moves with arrow keys, but Enter/Space is required to activate.
examples.code:
<Tabs.Root defaultValue="a">
  <Tabs.List activationMode="manual">
    <Tabs.Tab value="a">A</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab value="b">B</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Indicator />
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Panel value="a">A</Tabs.Panel>
  <Tabs.Panel value="b">B</Tabs.Panel>
</Tabs.Root>

examples.name: controlled
examples.code:
const [value, setValue] = useState('a');
<Tabs.Root value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
  <Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Tab value="a">A</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab value="b">B</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Indicator />
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Panel value="a">A</Tabs.Panel>
  <Tabs.Panel value="b">B</Tabs.Panel>
</Tabs.Root>
